summary: The derivative gives the instantaneous rate of change of a function — the slope of its graph. Differentiation applies a small set of rules term by term.
method: Choose the variable to differentiate with respect to (e.g. x).
Differentiate each term separately; constants differentiate to zero.
Apply the power rule d/dx(xⁿ) = n·xⁿ⁻¹ to power terms.
Use the product, quotient and chain rules for combinations of functions.

example.title: Differentiate a polynomial
steps: Differentiate x³ with the power rule: 3x².
